title: Paul Thomas Anderson Got His Oscars Due After a Best Picture Battle for the Ages
text: Paul Thomas Anderson won three Academy Awards at the 98th Oscars ceremony for his film One Battle After Another, which claimed Best Picture. He won for Best Director, Best Writer, and Best Producer—his first Oscar wins after an 0-for-11 losing streak spanning his previous six Oscar nominations. Upon accepting his directing award, Anderson remarked: You make a guy work hard for one of these, acknowledging the difficulty of finally securing recognition from the Academy. His previous acclaimed films like Boogie Nights, There Will Be Blood, and Phantom Thread had lost to other pictures. One Battle After Another emerged victorious in a competitive Best Picture race against Ryan Coogler's vampire film Sinners. Despite One Battle's dominant awards season performance, a surprising SAG Award loss to Sinners created uncertainty heading into the ceremony. Ultimately, the Anderson film prevailed with support across major categories, including Sean Penn's Best Supporting Actor win and supporting performances from Teyana Taylor and Benicio del Toro.
